description: Catholic Saints - Feast Days and Brief Biographies

01-01.name: Mary, Mother of God
01-01.brief: The Blessed Virgin Mary, honored as the Mother of God and model of faith.

01-17.name: St. Anthony the Great
01-17.brief: Desert father who founded monasticism in Egypt.

01-20.name: St. Sebastian
01-20.brief: Roman martyr who encouraged Christians during persecution.

01-28.name: St. Thomas Aquinas
01-28.brief: Doctor of the Church, wrote Summa Theologica.

02-02.name: Presentation of the Lord
02-02.brief: Jesus presented in the Temple, meeting Simeon and Anna.

02-11.name: Our Lady of Lourdes
02-11.brief: Mary appeared to St. Bernadette in Lourdes, France.

02-14.name: Sts. Cyril and Methodius
02-14.brief: Brothers who brought Christianity to the Slavic peoples.

02-22.name: Chair of St. Peter
02-22.brief: Celebrating the authority of Peter as first Pope.

03-17.name: St. Patrick
03-17.brief: Missionary who converted Ireland to Christianity.

03-19.name: St. Joseph
03-19.brief: Husband of Mary, foster father of Jesus, model of obedience.

03-25.name: Annunciation
03-25.brief: Angel Gabriel announces to Mary she will bear the Son of God.

04-23.name: St. George
04-23.brief: Martyr known for his courage and faith.

04-25.name: St. Mark
04-25.brief: Evangelist, author of the second Gospel.

05-01.name: St. Joseph the Worker
05-01.brief: Celebrating the dignity of human work.

05-13.name: Our Lady of Fatima
05-13.brief: Mary appeared to three shepherd children in Fatima, Portugal.

05-31.name: Visitation
05-31.brief: Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth, who recognizes her as Mother of God.

06-13.name: St. Anthony of Padua
06-13.brief: Franciscan preacher, Doctor of the Church.

06-24.name: Nativity of St. John the Baptist
06-24.brief: Birth of the forerunner of Christ.

06-29.name: Sts. Peter and Paul
06-29.brief: Apostles who founded the Church in Rome.

07-22.name: St. Mary Magdalene
07-22.brief: First witness to the Resurrection, apostle to the apostles.

07-25.name: St. James
07-25.brief: Apostle, brother of John, first apostle martyred.

08-06.name: Transfiguration
08-06.brief: Jesus revealed his divine glory to Peter, James, and John.

08-10.name: St. Lawrence
08-10.brief: Deacon martyred in Rome, known for his charity.

08-15.name: Assumption of Mary
08-15.brief: Mary taken body and soul into heavenly glory.

08-28.name: St. Augustine
08-28.brief: Doctor of the Church, author of Confessions.

09-08.name: Nativity of Mary
09-08.brief: Birth of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

09-14.name: Exaltation of the Holy Cross
09-14.brief: Celebrating the Cross as instrument of salvation.

09-29.name: Sts. Michael, Gabriel, Raphael
09-29.brief: The three archangels named in Scripture.

10-01.name: St. Therese of Lisieux
10-01.brief: Doctor of the Church, known for the Little Way.

10-04.name: St. Francis of Assisi
10-04.brief: Founder of Franciscans, lover of poverty and creation.

10-15.name: St. Teresa of Avila
10-15.brief: Doctor of the Church, Carmelite reformer.

11-01.name: All Saints
11-01.brief: Honoring all saints, known and unknown.

12-03.name: St. Francis Xavier
12-03.brief: Jesuit missionary to Asia, co-founder of Society of Jesus.

12-12.name: Our Lady of Guadalupe
12-12.brief: Mary appeared to Juan Diego in Mexico.

12-25.name: Christmas
12-25.brief: Birth of our Lord Jesus Christ.

12-28.name: Holy Innocents
12-28.brief: Children killed by Herod seeking Jesus.
